original_abstract:
This letter addresses the limitations of conventional linear energy harvesting (EH) models in simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) systems. While existing literature often assumes a constant radio frequency (RF)-to-DC conversion efficiency, practical EH circuits exhibit distinct non-linear characteristics due to hardware constraints and saturation effects. We propose a robust non-linear EH model based on the logistic function and validate its accuracy against experimental measurement data. Leveraging this model, we formulate a resource allocation problem aimed at maximizing the total harvested power at multiple energy receivers while satisfying specific signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) requirements for information receivers.

The resulting optimization problem is non-convex and comprises a sum-of-ratios objective function. To solve it, we apply a mathematical transformation to convert the objective into a subtractive form, enabling the development of an efficient iterative resource allocation algorithm. We further demonstrate that the inner loop of the algorithm can be solved optimally via semidefinite program (SDP) relaxation, proving that the relaxation is tight under general channel conditions. Numerical results highlight that the proposed design significantly outperforms baseline schemes based on linear models, preventing losses in harvested energy caused by model mismatch.
humanized_abstract:
This letter studies the limitations of the conventional linear energy harvesting (EH) models in the context of simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) systems. In fact, the majority of the works in the literature assume a fixed RF-to-DC conversion efficiency, while the real-world circuits have non-linear characteristics due to circuit implementation challenges and non-linear characteristics at high power levels. To handle this new challenge, a robust non-linear EH model using the logistic function is proposed and is validated from the experimental results. Finally, by exploiting the proposed non-linear model, the optimal resource allocation for multi-antenna EH system is studied and an efficient algorithm is proposed to optimize the total harvested power at the EH nodes and satisfy the desired SINR at the information decoding nodes.

The resulting optimization problem is non-convex, and the sum-of-ratios form of the objective function makes it particularly difficult to solve. The convex reformulation technique introduced in the earlier section can be leveraged to change the objective function to be subtractive, which allows us to propose a simple iterative resource allocation algorithm to solve the resulting problem efficiently. We can then optimally solve the inner loop of the proposed algorithm via SDP relaxation, for which we can prove the tightness of the relaxation under general channel conditions. Simulation results show that a large gain can be achieved in the proposed work as compared with linear models; without this proposed work, the model mismatch leads to the loss of energy harvest.

document.input:
This letter addresses the limitations of conventional linear energy harvesting (EH) models in simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) systems. While existing literature often assumes a constant radio frequency (RF)-to-DC conversion efficiency, practical EH circuits exhibit distinct non-linear characteristics due to hardware constraints and saturation effects.
